title: Screen a new dependency for typosquatting
description: Before adding a package, check its name, age, popularity, and maintainer against the intended well-known package to catch lookalike or hijacked names.
body: Steps: 1. Confirm the exact intended package name and its canonical source/repo. 2. Compare the candidate name for character swaps, added words, hyphen/scope differences, and homoglyphs against the real package. 3. Check the registry metadata: download counts, publish age, maintainer, and linked repository. 4. Verify the repo URL resolves to the legitimate project, not a fork created recently. 5. Record the verification before adding. Rollback point: before the package enters the lockfile. Validation: the package's name, maintainer, and repo match the intended well-known project and its popularity/age are consistent. Risk boundary: a low-download, newly-published lookalike of a popular package is a typosquat signal; deny the add and escalate rather than installing to 'see if it works'.
